Stemmatology workshop

JB Camps

2026-03-16

# if necessary:
# install.packages("stemmatology")
library("stemmatology")
library("ggplot2")
source("functions_new.R")

Textual criticism-based “PCC” method

For the documentation:

?`stemmatology-package`
# Import data
data = import.TEIApparatus_corr(file = "data/cliges_unified_fixed_unique.xml", appTypes = c("lexsem:weak", "lexsem:strong", "lexsem:nonsense", "absence", "order"))
# Check which witnesses have sufficient readings, and keep them
apply(X = is.na(data), MARGIN = 2, FUN = sum) / nrow(data)
##           A           B           C           M           P           R 
## 0.000000000 0.008547009 0.025641026 0.991452991 0.017094017 0.017094017 
##           S           T 
## 0.051282051 1.000000000
data = data[, c("A", "B", "C", "P", "R", "S")]
# Explore and analyse
select = PCC.Exploratory(data, omissionsAsReadings = FALSE, threshold = 0.1, ask = FALSE, pauseAtPlot = FALSE) #Threshold 0.10

## There is absolutely no conflicts in this database.
stemma = PCC.Stemma(select$database, verbose = TRUE, ask = FALSE)
## Now comparing group AB 
## Reading not assessable for the group AB at VL l-77_6 
## Reading not assessable for the group AB at VL l-77_7 
## Reading not assessable for the group AB at VL l-90_1 
## A has
##  0 severe disagreement(s),
##  3 benign disagreement(s),
##  4 omissions 
## towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## B has
##  0 severe disagreement(s),
##  10 benign disagreement(s),
##  3 omissions 
## towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## No ms inside group AB seems to be the model.
## We will proceed to a comparison with mss outside the group.
## C has 2 severe disagreement(s), 6 benign disagreement(s), 6 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## P has 0 severe disagreement(s), 10 benign disagreement(s), 4 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## R has 2 severe disagreement(s), 7 benign disagreement(s), 9 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## S has 0 severe disagreement(s), 30 benign disagreement(s), 15 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## No extant ms. at all seems to be the model of the group AB .
## It is presumably a lost ms.
## Now comparing group CR 
## Reading not assessable for the group CR at VL l-54_3 
## Reading not assessable for the group CR at VL l-68_3 
## Reading not assessable for the group CR at VL l-72_9 
## Reading not assessable for the group CR at VL l-90_4 
## C has
##  0 severe disagreement(s),
##  5 benign disagreement(s),
##  3 omissions 
## towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## R has
##  0 severe disagreement(s),
##  4 benign disagreement(s),
##  4 omissions 
## towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## No ms inside group CR seems to be the model.
## We will proceed to a comparison with mss outside the group.
## A has 3 severe disagreement(s), 5 benign disagreement(s), 6 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## B has 3 severe disagreement(s), 12 benign disagreement(s), 7 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## P has 0 severe disagreement(s), 9 benign disagreement(s), 2 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## S has 0 severe disagreement(s), 30 benign disagreement(s), 13 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## No extant ms. at all seems to be the model of the group CR .
## It is presumably a lost ms.
## Now comparing group PS 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-26_5 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-26_6 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-30_6 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-34_2 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-38_3 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-42_1 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-44_3 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-68_4 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-76_8 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-87_2 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-92_4 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-99_5 
## Reading not assessable for the group PS at VL l-99_9 
## P has
##  0 severe disagreement(s),
##  6 benign disagreement(s),
##  3 omissions 
## towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## S has
##  0 severe disagreement(s),
##  28 benign disagreement(s),
##  13 omissions 
## towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## No ms inside group PS seems to be the model.
## We will proceed to a comparison with mss outside the group.
## A has 3 severe disagreement(s), 4 benign disagreement(s), 8 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## B has 3 severe disagreement(s), 10 benign disagreement(s), 6 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## C has 2 severe disagreement(s), 6 benign disagreement(s), 6 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## R has 2 severe disagreement(s), 5 benign disagreement(s), 8 omissions towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## No extant ms. at all seems to be the model of the group PS .
## It is presumably a lost ms.
## Now comparing group {AB}{CR}{PS} 
## Reading not assessable for the group {AB}{CR}{PS} at VL l-31_2 
## {AB} has
##  0 severe disagreement(s),
##  3 benign disagreement(s),
##  4 omissions 
## towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## {CR} has
##  0 severe disagreement(s),
##  2 benign disagreement(s),
##  4 omissions 
## towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## {PS} has
##  0 severe disagreement(s),
##  1 benign disagreement(s),
##  1 omissions 
## towards the virtual model.
## It does not seem to be the model
## No ms inside group {AB}{CR}{PS} seems to be the model.
## We will proceed to a comparison with mss outside the group.
## [1] "There are no other manuscript left in the database.\n"
## No extant ms. at all seems to be the model of the group {AB}{CR}{PS} .
## It is presumably a lost ms.

Cladistics

library("phangorn")
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : ape
data = phyDat(as.matrix(t(data)), type = "USER", levels = 0:10, ambiguity=NA)

Neighbour-joining

Neighbour joining,

dm <- dist.ml(data)
tree <- NJ(dm)
plot(tree, "unrooted")

tree <- UNJ(dm)
plot(tree, "unrooted")

Maximum parsimony

mm_start <- random.addition(data)
# Applying the pratchet method for maximum parsimony
# Default, fitch + SPR
mm_tree <- pratchet(data, start = mm_start, minit = 1000, maxit = 10000, all = TRUE, trace = 0, method = "fitch", rearrangements = "SPR", perturbation = "ratchet") # "The "fitch" algorithm only works correct for binary trees"
# Alternative
#mm_tree <- pratchet(data, start = mm_start, minit = 1000, maxit = 10000, all = TRUE, trace = 0, method = "sankoff", rearrangements = "SPR", perturbation = "ratchet")
mm_tree
## 
## Phylogenetic tree with 6 tips and 4 internal nodes.
## 
## Tip labels:
##   A, B, C, P, R, S
## Node labels:
##   1, 0.898, 0.736, 0.98
## 
## Unrooted; no branch length.
parsimony(mm_tree, data)
## [1] 143
# Determining branch length
mm_tree <- acctran(mm_tree, data)
# Eliminating too short branches, to integrate polytomies/multifurcations
# we prune away internal edges of length tol (default = 1e-08), so our trees may contain multifurcations.
mm_tree  <- di2multi(mm_tree)
# In case of several competing trees
#plot(consensus(mm_tree), "unrooted", main="Unrooted consensus tree")
plotBS(mm_tree, type = "unrooted")

plot(mm_tree, "unrooted", main="Unrooted tree")

Maximum likelihood

# test different models
mt <- modelTest(data, model=c("JC", "F81", "K80", "HKY", "SYM", "GTR"), control = pml.control(trace = 0))
mt
##        Model df    logLik      AIC         AICw     AICc        AICcw      BIC
## 1        SYM 63 -729.1530 1584.306 5.867140e-74 1736.457 2.065734e-66 1758.323
## 2      SYM+I 64 -729.1530 1586.306 2.158401e-74 1746.306 1.500995e-68 1763.085
## 3   SYM+G(4) 64 -729.5122 1587.024 1.507028e-74 1747.024 1.048017e-68 1763.804
## 4 SYM+G(4)+I 65 -729.5122 1589.024 5.544046e-75 1757.260 6.277726e-71 1768.566
## 5        GTR 67 -557.0084 1248.017 6.204535e-01 1433.976 9.953848e-01 1433.082
## 6      GTR+I 68 -557.0040 1250.008 2.292474e-01 1445.508 3.117628e-03 1437.836
## 7   GTR+G(4) 68 -557.7397 1251.479 1.098500e-01 1446.979 1.493894e-03 1439.307
## 8 GTR+G(4)+I 69 -557.7388 1253.478 4.044917e-02 1459.009 3.647764e-06 1444.068
fit_mt <- pml_bb(mt, control = pml.control(trace = 0))
#fitGTR <- pml_bb(data, model="GTR")

And now bootstrapping,

bs <- bootstrap.pml(fit_mt, bs=100, optNni=TRUE, control = pml.control(trace = 0))
plotBS(midpoint(fit_mt$tree), p = .5, type="p", digits=2, main="Ultrafast bootstrap")

plotBS(midpoint(fit_mt$tree), bs, p = 50, type="p", main="Standard bootstrap")

#plotBS(midpoint(fit_mt$tree), bs, p = 50, type="p", digits=0, method = "TBE", main="Transfer bootstrap")
